title: Cat Squinting and Holding Head Low: Triage Guide & Next Steps
meta_desc: Is your cat squinting and hanging their head? Learn what these signs mean, the urgency level, and why you should contact a vet today.
content_html:
Understanding Your Cat's Symptoms

When a cat squints their eyes and holds their head low, it is a significant sign of physical discomfort or pain. This combination of symptoms often suggests that your cat is trying to protect their eyes from light or is experiencing a headache or pressure. Common causes include corneal ulcers, eye infections, high blood pressure, or even neurological issues. Because cats are experts at masking pain, these visible signs indicate that their discomfort has reached a level they can no longer hide.

Is It an Emergency?

The urgency level for these symptoms is Medium. While it may not always be a life-threatening crisis at this exact moment, eye conditions can deteriorate rapidly, potentially leading to permanent vision loss if left untreated. Additionally, a lowered head can indicate systemic illness or lethargy. You should contact your veterinarian or an urgent care clinic to have your cat evaluated within the next 12 to 24 hours. If you notice symptoms like gasping for breath, stumbling, or sudden blindness, seek emergency care immediately.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Taking a clear, high-resolution photo of your cat’s eyes and their posture can be an invaluable tool for your veterinary team. A photo captures the exact state of the eye—including any discharge, cloudiness, or pupil irregularities—before the cat is stressed by travel. This allows the triage nurse to better assess the severity of the situation over the phone and determine how quickly your pet needs to be seen by a doctor.

faqs.question: Could my cat have a scratch on their eye?
faqs.answer: Yes, a corneal ulcer or scratch is a very common reason for squinting. It is extremely painful and requires a special dye test from a vet to diagnose and treat properly.

faqs.question: Does a lowered head always mean my cat is in pain?
faqs.answer: Generally, yes. Cats typically hold their heads high and alert. Holding the head low is a 'guarding' posture that suggests they are feeling weak, nauseated, or in pain.

faqs.question: Can I use human eye drops on my cat?
faqs.answer: No, you should never use human eye medications on a cat without a veterinarian's approval, as some ingredients can cause severe irritation or even life-threatening reactions in felines.
